setting.note: A writer's hunt for his vanished father's lost bicycle becomes a walk back through twentieth-century Taiwan. The map centres on old central Taipei: Zhonghua Road and the vast Zhonghua Shopping Bazaar the family lived and tailored suits in, and the Wanhua-Bangka district of temples, antique stalls and bicycle shops. The search reaches back to the Japanese colonial years and, far to the south-west, to the forests of the Burma campaign of the Second World War.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Wu Ming-Yi, first published in Chinese as Danche shiqie ji, 2015; English translation by Darryl Sterk, 2017. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Stolen Bicycle, Wu Ming-Yi (2015). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The green of a bicycle's aged enamel and the island's mountain forests. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
